Title: Fraud Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like


Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.


Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?


Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.
Technical Proficiency – You are highly skilled in SQL and Python for data analysis and strategy development.
Domain Expertise – You have a solid understanding of payment fraud dynamics and experience working with real-time decisioning environments.
Communication – You possess excellent communication skills and are fluent in English.
Nice to Have – Experience with Machine Learning, Data Science, PySpark, or GraphQL knowledge is a significant plus.


CareAbout: how we support your impact

About

We move fast, and evolution never stops. It's a fun ride, but it can be challenging. To make sure our people truly thrive, we're committed to making their lives easier, both in the office and out in the world. That's why we created CareAbout:


❤️ Health (Private insurance for you and your family, psychological support with Serenis, mental health workshops)
💰 Financial resources (Stock Option Plan, Meal vouchers, Relocation support if you're moving countries)
⚙️ Growth and development (Professional development programs, Internal mobility, Language courses with Preply)
🌱 Flexibility (Unlimited PTO, Hybrid working policy*, Flexible working hours)
👨‍👩‍👧 Family (Enhanced parental leave, Additional leave for child sickness)
We embrace three days per week in-office (Tuesday and Thursday + 1 of your choice), with the option to request extra remote time.


Equal opportunity employer

At Satispay, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds. This opportunity is open to everyone, regardless - for instance - of race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, and national origin. Join us in a workplace where everyone belongs!

Learn More About Us

Our values and pillars aren't just fancy words on a page - they really shape everything we do. Explore them here.

By submitting this application, I acknowledge that I have read and understood the content of the Privacy Policy

Title: Data Analyst (Python, SQL, Power BI)
Description:
Mission

We are looking for a detail-oriented Data Analyst to transform raw data into actionable insights that support business decision-making. The role focuses on analyzing large datasets using SQL and Python and presenting findings through Power BI dashboards. You will partner closely with stakeholders to understand business needs and deliver data-driven solutions. This position requires strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and the ability to communicate insights clearly. You will also contribute to improving data quality, reporting processes, and overall data maturity across the organization.

Key Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy
Analyze datasets using SQL and Python to identify trends and insights
Build and maintain interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs and deliver solutions
Monitor data quality and suggest improvements to data processes
Document methodologies, analysis, and key findings


Requirements


Proven experience as a Data Analyst or similar role
Strong skills in SQL and Python (e.g., pandas, numpy)
Experience with Power BI for dashboards and reporting
Solid understanding of data modelling and ETL concepts
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
Ability to translate data into business insights


How we work: At PPG, we bring teams together to accelerate collaboration and career development, with in-person work Monday through Thursday and the opportunity to work remotely on Flexible Fridays. Schedules may vary by location.

About Us

PPG: WE PROTECT AND BEAUTIFY THE WORLD™

Through leadership in innovation, sustainability and color, PPG helps customers in industrial, transportation, consumer products, and construction markets and aftermarkets to enhance more surfaces in more ways than does any other company. To learn more, visit www.ppg.com and follow @PPG on X.

The PPG Way 2030

We are customer champions

Proactive. Bold. Trustworthy. Everything we do starts with our customers. We listen, move fast and don’t stop until we solve their biggest challenges. When our customers win, we all grow.

We act with purpose and speed

Agile. Data-driven. Empowered. We take smart risks to stay ahead of the competition. We work proactively with agility, using quality data to develop solutions that create value.

We are excellent operators

Productive. Collaborative. Accountable. No matter our role, we identify problems, take ownership and always bring solutions. We are both proactive and responsive to drive continuous improvement and deliver results. We support our frontline, the faces of PPG to our customers.

We compete to win

Future-focused. Driven. Ambitious. We are passionate about growing our business and winning with our customers. We deliver results, embrace new technologies and leverage agility and speed as strengths.

We are PPG proud

Strong. United. Passionate. We work safely, act with integrity and value our diverse perspectives. We celebrate achievements and take pride in the positive impact we create together to protect and beautify the world.

At PPG we use AI in the hiring process to make the process more efficient. AI tools do not make hiring decisions. You can learn more by going to https://careers.ppg.com/us/en/candidate-resources.

PPG provides equal opportunity to all candidates and employees. We offer an opportunity to grow and develop your career in an environment that provides a fulfilling workplace for employees, creates an environment for continuous learning, and embraces the ideas and diversity of others. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, pregnancy, race, color, creed, religion, national origin, age, disability status, marital status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression.

If you need an adjustment due to a disability, please email recruiting@ppg.com.

PPG pay ranges and benefits can vary by location which allows us to compensate employees competitively in different geographic markets. PPG considers several factors in making compensation decisions including, but not limited to, skill sets, experience and training, qualifications and education, licensure and certifications, and other organizational needs. Other incentives may apply.

Our employee benefits programs are designed to support the health and well-being of our employees. Any insurance coverages and benefits will be in accordance with the terms and conditions of the applicable plans and associated governing plan documents. Benefits will be discussed with you by your recruiter during the hiring process.

PPG values your feedback on our recruiting process. We encourage you to visit Glassdoor.com and provide feedback on the process, so that we can do better today than yesterday.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Company Description 

Ona Therapeutics is a biotech company located in Barcelona (Parc Científic de Barcelona, PCB), focused on developing first-in-class antibody drug conjugates (ADCs) for advanced cancer treatment. By partnering with leading academics and clinicians, we identify novel therapeutic targets from the unique biology of treatment refractory advanced cancer patients, to provide effective new treatments for patients with no remaining alternatives. 

 

Scope 

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst with a strong scientific background to support data-driven decision-making across our project portfolio and strategic initiatives. 




This role sits at the intersection of science, data, and project strategy, supporting the Head of Project & Business Management in transforming complex biological, clinical, and industry data into structured insights that guide internal decision-making. 




You will play a key role in strengthening how the company uses data to evaluate priorities, track programs, and understand the competitive landscape. 

 

Role and Responsibilities  

Business Intelligence & Data Analysis  

Develop a deep understanding of the biotech/pharma landscape, including competitors, modalities, and therapeutic trends 
Gather, structure, and analyse scientific, clinical, and regulatory data (clinical trials, publications, databases, etc.) 
Translate complex datasets into clear insights, summaries, and decision-support materials 
Support portfolio and project teams by providing data-driven insights for prioritisation and planning 
Contribute to the definition of key analytical frameworks and indicators for project and portfolio evaluation 
Support the Head of Project & Business Management in preparing data-driven analyses for internal decision-making 




Strategic Insight & External Analysis 

Support evaluation of external opportunities (assets, indications, collaborations) through structured data analysis 
Contribute to competitive intelligence and landscape mapping 
Assist in preparing analytical inputs for strategic discussions and presentations 




Scientific Integration 

Participate in internal scientific and project meetings 
Collaborate closely with R&D and project management teams to understand data needs 
Contribute to a data-driven culture across project execution and decision-making 




Tools & Data Systems 

Support the structuring and maintenance of internal BI and project intelligence databases 
Work with scientific databases, literature sources, and internal data systems 
Contribute to improving how data is collected, structured, and visualised across projects 

 

Qualifications Requirements  

PhD in relevant scientific fields (Biology, Biochemistry, Chemistry, Pharmacology, Biomedical Sciences, etc.) 
Relevant professional experience in business intelligence highly analytical roles within biotech, consulting, venture capital, or technology transfer, demonstrating strong ability to structure complex problems, interpret data-driven insights, and support evidence-based decision-making. 
Strong interest in biotech, data analysis, and project/portfolio decision-making 
Experience in oncology, immunology, drug-development or related 

 

Skills 

Strong analytical, structured, and systematic mindset  
Excellent critical thinking and problem-solving abilities  
Data-driven with a hypothesis-oriented approach  
Ability to translate complex scientific and clinical data into clear, actionable insights  
Ability to simplify complex information and extract key messages  
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills  
Excellent communication and presentation skills  
Intellectual curiosity and proactive mindset  
Ownership mindset with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment

﻿ 

What we offer 

A work environment, culture and passionate team in the oncology sector. 
A unique opportunity to work in an innovative and dynamic environment. 
Internal benefits such as flexible remuneration, flexible working hours, events along the year... 

 

To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter to hr@ona-therapeutics.com 

We look forward to reviewing your application and discovering how your skills and experience align with our company’s needs.

Title: Process & Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Process & Data Analyst for an important software company located in Barcelona.




Responsibilities:

Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset, identify business questions, performance gaps, and improvement opportunities, and translate them into clear analytical approaches.
Support GTM initiatives by delivering insights on business performance, operational efficiency, financial drivers, and strategic KPIs.
Design, build, and maintain dashboards, reports, and analytical models that track performance against targets and support leadership decision-making.
Conduct ad hoc and deep-dive analyses to explain trends, variances, risks, and opportunities, and to evaluate the impact of business initiatives.
Work closely with members of the RevX team and business stakeholders to understand their objectives, define relevant metrics, and ensure analytical outputs are aligned with decision needs.
Partner with data engineering, finance, and other analytics teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and scalability of reporting solutions.
Synthesize complex analyses into clear, concise narratives and deliver presentations that translate data into actionable insights for senior leaders.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of analytical frameworks, tools, and best practices within the RevX team.




Requirements:

Master’s degree in Analytics, Data Science, Economics, Statistics, Engineering, Business, or a related quantitative field.
5+ years of experience in data analysis, business analytics, or performance management roles.
Strong experience with SQL and at least one analytical programming language (Python or equivalent).
Experience building dashboards and reports using BI tools (Power BI preferred).
Strong analytical thinking with demonstrated ability to structure ambiguous problems.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical audiences.
Proven ability to collaborate effectively in cross-functional teams.
Fluent in English.




We Offer:

Responsibilities:

🔹 Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics 🔹

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join a Data & Insights team within an international transformation environment.

In this role, you will play a key part in shaping how data supports decision-making across the organisation, working at the intersection of advanced analytics, business performance, and emerging AI capabilities.

You will contribute to high-impact initiatives across commercial performance, operational analytics, finance insights, and the evolution of self-service analytics and data products.

This is a highly business-facing role, ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex datasets into clear insights, building meaningful KPIs, supporting senior stakeholders, and exploring how AI can improve productivity, insight generation, and data discoverability.

If you enjoy working with SQL, Python, Power BI, large datasets, business stakeholders, and AI-driven analytics tools, this could be a great fit.







💻 What you’ll do

🔹 Translate complex data into clear, structured, and actionable outputs for business and senior stakeholders

🔹 Prepare reports, dashboards, and performance analysis suitable for senior management and executive-level decision-making

🔹 Work closely with business stakeholders to understand needs, define relevant KPIs, and build performance frameworks

🔹 Analyse business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational efficiency, customer behaviour, and commercial performance

🔹 Support and enhance existing reporting solutions, including Power BI dashboards and performance reports

🔹 Contribute to the design and evolution of self-service analytics solutions and data products

🔹 Collaborate with Data Engineers and platform teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and usability

🔹 Identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities through data analysis and business context

🔹 Explore and experiment with AI-driven tools and approaches to improve insight generation, productivity, and data accessibility

🔹 Help improve data adoption across the organisation by making insights clear, accessible, and actionable

🔹 Communicate complex analytical topics in a clear and structured way to both technical and non-technical audiences

🔹 Work in a fast-paced, transformation-driven environment with cross-functional teams







💡 Must Have




🔹 6+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data & Insights, or similar roles

🔹 Strong experience working with large datasets and analytical tools

🔹 Strong proficiency in SQL

🔹 Experience with Python for data analysis, automation, or analytical workflows

🔹 Strong experience with Power BI or similar BI / data visualisation tools

🔹 Proven ability to turn complex data into actionable business insights

🔹 Experience building dashboards, reports, KPIs, and performance analysis for business users

🔹 Good understanding of business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational KPIs, commercial performance, or finance metrics

🔹 Experience working with stakeholders at different levels, including senior business stakeholders

🔹 Ability to communicate complex topics in a clear, structured, and business-oriented way

🔹 Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and attention to detail

🔹 Curiosity and willingness to experiment with new technologies, especially AI-driven analytics tools

🔹 Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast-moving, and transformation-driven environments

🔹 Intermediate English level or above







✨ Nice to Have




🔹 Experience preparing reporting for senior management, executive committees, or management committees

🔹 Experience contributing to self-service analytics, data products, or reusable reporting frameworks

🔹 Experience using AI tools to improve productivity, automate analysis, generate insights, or support data discoverability

🔹 Experience collaborating with Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers, or platform teams

🔹 Experience with data quality, data governance, data modelling, or analytics engineering practices

🔹 Experience with Tableau, Qlik, Looker, Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or similar tools

🔹 Background in commercial analytics, operational analytics, finance analytics, pricing, revenue management, or performance tracking







🏢 Hybrid model - 2 days on site per week

🤝 People first – diverse and inclusive culture in an international environment.

🚀 Help shape how data supports strategic decision-making across a large international organisation.

🧠 Explore the evolution of AI-driven analytics, productivity tools, and data discoverability.

Title: Finance Data Analyst
Description:
As you contemplate your future, you might be asking yourself, what's the next step? Start your journey with us!

We are now looking for a Data Analyst for Finance to join our team in Barcelona (hybrid). The Finance Data Analytics team has been transformative during the last two years by helping our stakeholders succeed in their mission. If you're ready to soar, we're ready to take you there.

Why eDreams ODIGEO

Join the world's leading travel subscription platform

and one of the largest e-commerce businesses in Europe.


Millions of customers every year across 44 markets – 5 brands – 7.7M members in Prime since launching in 2017.
More than 100 million searches per day on our websites - more than 6 billion AI daily predictions
Over 1700 employees – More than 60 different nationalities - 99% permanent contracts - 36.3 average age



We're the world’s leading travel subscription platform. We pioneered Prime, the first and largest travel subscription program, which has topped over 7.7 million members since launching in 2017. Prime members are subscribed to global travel, gaining access to a comprehensive multi-product offering for all their travel needs—including hotels, rail, flights, dynamic packages and car rental, among others— compounded by industry-leading flexibility features and exclusive, member-only benefits. This entire Prime experience is powered by a proprietary, industry-leading AI platform that delivers a smarter, hyper-personalized service and comprehensive travel experience globally to its members.

What you will do:

The Role's Key Responsibilities and Tasks

As an eDOer, you will have clear objectives, great challenges and a clear overview of how your work contributes to the global company project and its customers. As a Finance Data Analyst you will be in charge of:


Responsible for execution of key analysis, being the data entry point for the business team and including taking ownership of the different data sources from the business side. Understand the business needs, gather the data and present the key insights: “Contribute to the Finance strategic roadmap with numbers”
Elaborate business cases to support strategic decisions
Create dashboards and tracking: master the internal Business Intelligence tools to contribute to the monitoring and tracking of our business KPIs. Work with data engineering and data science teams to be able to leverage all our internal data
Contribute to the delivery of Finance strategic projects: participate in the delivery of the key internal projects, giving support from the business side to ensure progress and effective delivery of the desired outcomes.



what do you need to succeed:

Good to have

Bring your unique perspective, speak up, and offer disruptive solutions. You'll have the opportunity to learn and grow while making a real impact on our team. Here's what you need to succeed:


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative discipline (Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, etc.),
2-4 years experience in a role with strong analytical component
Strong knowledge of SQL is a requirement,
Python programming skills (mostly for data extraction and data manipulation),
Advanced Office skills. Strong experience with analytical tools, ideally with the Google toolset (spreadsheets, google docs, etc)
Strong analytical skills, fast-learner, with high attention to detail and focused on results and productivity
Business Proficiency in English and fluent in Spanish
A self-starter and team player, who also enjoys having fun!



It would be a plus if you have:


Google Analytics knowledge (as well as Looker Studio),
Previous experience in programming,
Knowledge on data structure and data sets management (as Google Cloud Platform),
SAP knowledge.



Preferred Qualifications


Experience with Applied AI Tools: Demonstrated comfort using practical AI tools such as Gemini, or other AI-powered assistants.
Experimentation Mindset: Curiosity and eagerness to explore, experiment with, and integrate emerging AI-driven solutions into workflows.
AI-Enhanced Problem Solving: Ability to effectively leverage AI tools to streamline processes, and enhance productivity.
Adaptability and Learning Agility: Enthusiastic about continuously learning and quickly adapting to new AI features and capabilities.



and what's in it for you?

The best talent deserves the best benefits

At eDO, we want you to be a part of our success story and great culture. Here's what we offer:


A rewarding Compensation package! 💥 Prime Plus membership, competitive salary and benefits package, including flexible benefits, performance-based bonuses, birthday day off, discounts and partnerships, relocation support and the possibility of choosing your equipment and, even better, keeping it for free after 3 years.
Continuous learning to fuel your growth and explore new horizons! 📚 Learn and grow with free Coursera access, soft skills workshops, tech training, leadership development, and more. Plus, enjoy a great onboarding program.
Grow opportunities to empower your career, and unleash your potential! 🚀 Personalised career paths and the eVOLVE Program will help you discover, grow, and thrive. Internal mobility opportunities let you pursue horizontal career changes and promotions.
Your Well-being is our priority. Embrace Freedom and Flexibility! 💙 At eDO, we value flexibility, employee care, and transparency. We offer a hybrid home-office model focused on outcome, not time-in-seat. You'll be able to find the right work-personal life balance that suits you best.
Work hard, party hard! We believe in having fun and connecting with colleagues! 🎉 Join eDO for after-work events, paddle tournaments, parties, and more. Create communities based on your passions, like sports and music. Come to work as you are, with no dress code, and enjoy free fruit, coffee, and tea at our offices.
Enjoy a dynamic and healthy environment! 💫 Be innovative, take risks, and share your ideas. Our diverse and open-minded teams support high performance, learning, and growth. You'll work in an Agile mindset environment with recognition at our core.



Wanna take a peek into what it's like to work at eDO? Follow us on LinkedIn and discover more about life at eDO!

If you are ready for a career opportunity with unmatched benefits, continuous learning, and a supportive work-life balance, look no further! Take your career to new destinations by applying now and help our diverse, inclusive, and passionate team shape the future of travel.

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status. Our people are the key of our success. Each one of them is unique in their own way and the respect for their uniqueness is and will always be a passion and motivation. Don’t meet every requirement? If you’re excited about this role but you do not feel your experience aligns perfectly with all the requirements we strongly encourage you to apply nevertheless.

Apply now!

For one of our key clients, we are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join a high-impact environment, supporting multiple priority projects requiring strong data expertise and autonomy.



Key Responsibilities


Design and lead data analysis initiatives using advanced SQL
Gather and challenge requirements with regional Data Officers
Translate business needs into clear technical specifications
Work on ETL processes and data pipelines within AWS environments (backend-focused)
Perform cross-domain data exploration and identify optimal solutions
Ensure data quality, structure, and consistency across datasets
Proactively suggest improvements and optimizations
Work independently as a senior contributor within the team

4-5 years of experience as Data Analyst

Hands-on experience working with AWS
Ability to work autonomously and engage with stakeholders
Professional level of English


Nice to Have


Experience with Informatica PowerCenter
Ability to support light development tasks when required


Position Details

Full time - 40 hours per week
Start date: ASAP (end of June / early July)

Title: Financial Data Analyst (con IA)
Description:
Estamos buscando un perfil enfocado en análisis financiero y elaboración de informes, para incorporarse al área de Assurance (auditoría, forensic y proyectos especiales).

El candidato trabajará junto a un equipo de auditores senior, por lo que no se requiere un perfil altamente especializado en contabilidad avanzada, pero sí una base sólida en finanzas y capacidad de aprendizaje rápido.

Responsabilidades principales


Elaboración de informes financieros, incluyendo informes periciales y especiales
Participación en proyectos de due diligence y auditoría
Análisis de estados financieros consolidados
Redacción de informes con alta calidad narrativa y claridad analítica
Apoyo al equipo de auditoría en el análisis de información financiera compleja
Identificación de riesgos, desviaciones y puntos críticos en la información financiera



Requisitos imprescindibles


Formación en Economía, ADE o similar
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en contabilidad, auditoría o despacho financiero
Capacidad analítica y atención al detalle
Mentalidad de aprendizaje y crecimiento profesional
Interés por desarrollarse en el área de auditoría y análisis financiero
Experiencia o conocimientos en Data o herramientas de IA aplicada a análisis financiero
Interés por la tecnología (sin necesidad de programar)



Perfil ideal

Buscamos una persona con:


Alta capacidad analítica y estructurada
Motivación por aprender y desarrollarse en auditoría
Capacidad para investigar, interpretar datos y extraer conclusiones

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
Location: We are flexible! Come and join us either in Hamburg, Berlin or Barcelona.

Freenow empowers smarter mobility decisions helping people to move freely and cities to thrive.

We are looking for a Product Data Analyst (m/f/d) to collaborate with our product squads, using data to build a flawless product experience. You will join the Product Data Team and work closely with PMs, designers, UX researchers, developers, and other data functions to discover new product opportunities, validate hypotheses, and launch valuable features to our users.

Be ready to work in a multinational, diverse, highly motivated and collaborative team of passionate analysts who strive for excellence and like to have fun. Are you ready for your next ride?

YOUR DAILY ADVENTURES WILL INCLUDE:


Providing expertise and collaborating with stakeholders to generate new product feature ideas and improvements, and also estimating the potential impact of planned projects.
Continuously engaging with the product squads to gain an understanding of the problems that need to be solved, gathering requirements, and defining the best deliverables to match the business needs.
Having full ownership of your analysis, from ideation to communication of results and making recommendations to all levels of the company including senior management and prioritization committees.
Developing and evaluating A/B test experiments or other analyses to measure and report the performance of released features, ensuring that we are launching the correct changes and features in our apps.
Working closely with our developers and data engineers to create and capture data that is aligned with the business logic and allows modeling and analysis.
Being involved throughout the complete product lifecycle in order to guarantee a data-driven process.
Bringing new insights and championing them using your analyses, dashboards, and good storytelling! We value ideas over hierarchy, and there's always a new path to follow if your idea is convincing and well-backed with data.
You will have the opportunity to steer our products, which kind of features should be developed, and which changes should be made to solve hundreds of thousands of users' daily needs.


TO BE SUCCESSFUL IN THIS ROLE:


You have curiosity in your essence, which motivates you to answer WHY things happened, not only scratching the surface, but aiming for well-founded actionable results
Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Engineering, or other fields with a strong mathematical-statistical background
3+ years experience in working as a Data Analyst in a data-driven analytics environment, preferably within tech product organizations, having direct contact with stakeholders.
Exceptional analytical skills, lateral thinking, and proven experience in solving highly challenging problems.
Very good knowledge of SQL, databases, and statistical programming tools (e.g. Python, R) so that exploratory analyses can be performed, reports created and ETLs maintained
Good understanding and hands-on experience in analyzing and interpreting A/B test results, backed by statistical knowledge.
Experience in working with data visualisation tools (e.g.Tableau, Qlikview) and event tracking (e.g. Mixpanel, GA, Firebase)
You are able to communicate findings in a concise and clear manner to a wide variety of stakeholders to support data-driven decision making by using data visualisation and storytelling and have excellent English communication skills.


BENEFITS & PERKS IN A NUTSHELL:


Flexible working arrangements
LinkedIn Learning
Sabbatical & special leave policies
WeRoad partnership
Birthday, 24th + 31st December off
Short term EU work policy
Mobility Credit
Health Insurance
Employee assistance program


Plus more local benefits depending on your work location!

DIVERSITY, EQUITY & INCLUSION:

Freenow is an equal opportunity employer and we consider qualified applicants regardless of race, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or age.

We want you to grow and evolve, bring your true self to work.

ABOUT US:

Freenow by Lyft empowers smarter mobility decisions, helping people to move freely and cities to thrive. Through our multi-mobility app, we feature broad options for everyone across 9 European markets and over 180 cities. Millions of passengers can access services including taxis, private hire vehicles, carsharing, car rental, e-scooters, e-bikes, e-mopeds, and public transport within a single app.

In July 2025 Freenow was acquired by Lyft, a global mobility platform, which connects riders and drivers for billions of rides across North America and Europe. Together, Freenow and Lyft are creating a more caring and connected world, with transportation for everyone. We are a global, diverse, highly motivated, and collaborative team that strives for excellence and likes to have fun. Ready for your next ride?

Title: Data Analyst - Sales Business Administration (F/M)
Description:
We are hiring a Data Analyst and Data Scientist to join our Worldwide Sales Business Administration team!

Are you looking to flourish in an innovative and dynamic environment where you constantly learn to solve new business challenges and work on projects and problems that have never been encountered before?

Are you passionate about building business relationships to support a developing inside sales and online business to achieve its goals and revenue targets?

We have an exciting role to join our Sales Business Administration and Operations team in Barcelona Spain where you will work directly with the Global Inside Sales team to provide Data Analysis as well as assistance and sales transaction support for all customer engagements.

Your Missions


Summarize sales data and insights using dashboards and intelligence gathered across the business
Development and support of business-critical data models and dashboards necessary for supporting the Business Development Hub Inside Sales and Sales Operations functions
Reveal hidden data value and assist operational teams through to senior management with discovery analytics, predictions and recommendations
Document and deliver data summary, comprehensive insights and business intelligence necessary to support the growth of the Business Development Hub organization.
Provide sales transaction support across the entire customer lifecycle, ensuring accurate and timely processing of opportunities, orders, renewals, and revenue bookings.
Monitor and support forecast and pipeline accuracy across sales teams, brands, industries, and customer engagement models.
Leverage KPIs, reporting, and data analytics to identify risks, opportunities, and actionable recommendations for Sales Management.
Ensure data quality and consistency across forecasting, pipeline, customer, and opportunity management processes.
Analyse sales performance, pipeline trends, customer activity, and revenue metrics to identify business improvement opportunities.
Ensure business documentation, opportunity records, WIN Plans, Score Cards, and sales processes remain compliant with company guidelines and standards.
Work collaboratively with Sales, Finance, Operations, and Management teams to drive operational excellence and continuous process improvement.


Your qualification


Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related discipline.
Experience in Sales Operations, Business Administration, Revenue Operations, Sales Support, or a similar business environment preferred.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret business data and provide meaningful recommendations.
Experience creating reports, dashboards, and performance metrics using Excel, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytical tools.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to maintaining high levels of data quality and accuracy.
Eager to stay on the edge of knowledge & know-how as regards machine learning / deep learning technologies, self-learning profile.
Experience building and optimizing ‘Big Data’ data pipelines, architectures and data sets.
A successful history of manipulating, processing and extracting value from large disconnected datasets.
Expertise SQL development using stored procedures & frameworks.
Experience working with large-scale data processing and storage as well as coding with Python scripting
Ability to build strong working relationships with sales teams, management, and cross-functional stakeholders.
Strong communication skills with the ability to present insights and recommendations clearly and effectively.
Demonstrated problem-solving capability and a proactive approach to identifying operational improvements.
Knowledge of CRM systems, forecasting processes, pipeline management, and sales performance measurement is highly desirable.
Enthusiasm for combining business operations excellence with data analysis to support business growth and revenue achievement.
Language: English mandatory, Spanish preferable but not essential


What's In It For You

Join one of Europe’s largest and most influential software companies.

Gain broad exposure to diverse industry experiences and cutting-edge IT technologies.

Thrive in an international work environment alongside talented colleagues from around the world.

Benefit from a supportive atmosphere designed for both personal and professional growth.

Inclusion statement

As a game-changer in sustainable technology and innovation, Dassault Systèmes is striving to build more inclusive and diverse teams across the globe. We believe that our people are our number one asset and we want all employees to feel empowered to bring their whole selves to work every day. It is our goal that our people feel a sense of pride and a passion for belonging. As a company leading change, it’s our responsibility to foster opportunities for all people to participate in a harmonized Workforce of the Future.

Title: GESTOR/A DATA SCIENTIST RIESGO OPERACIONAL (MAD/BCN)
Description:
Descripción del puesto:

¿Qué proyectos desarrollamos?

 

La misión de la dirección de Riesgo Operacional es asegurar la identificación, medición, evaluación, gestión, mitigación y reporting del riesgo operacional del Grupo CaixaBank. 


Buscamos una persona capaz de llevar a cabo el análisis de grandes volúmenes de datos para identificar patrones y tendencias que puedan indicar riesgos potenciales, desarrollar modelos predictivos mediante la utilización de técnicas de aprendizaje automático y modelos estadísticos para predecir eventos de riesgo y su impacto en la organización, proponer estrategias de mitigación para mejorar la resiliencia organizacional, y automatizar la detección y gestión de riesgos mediante soluciones tecnológicas. 


Los proyectos que asumirás en la posición son: 

Colaboración en el diseño e implementación del nuevo modelo de datos de pérdidas operacionales que permita la captura de nuevas fuentes de información y su tratamiento avanzado mediante:
Desarrollo de modelos predictivos mediante la utilización de técnicas de aprendizaje automático y modelos estadísticos para predecir eventos de riesgo y su impacto en la organización.
Desarrollo y evaluación de modelos híbridos, que combinan algoritmos de aprendizaje automático (ML) y técnicas de estadística avanzada, para la estimación de pérdidas operacionales, abordando desafíos como la escasez y heterogeneidad de datos, la optimización de distribuciones con colas pesadas y la estimación de eventos extremos.
Colaboración en la migración de procesos a Google Cloud (BigQuery, DBT, Vertex AI), diseñando pipelines escalables que aseguren la trazabilidad, reproducibilidad y eficiencia de los modelos predictivos, así como la automatización del reporting.
Colaboración en la preparación de datos para el reporting interno, principalmente en el Comité de Riesgo Operacional, así como involucración en informes y en comunicación con la Alta Dirección, Validación Interna, Auditoría y Supervisores.
Interrelación con los otros equipos de gestión especializados en pérdidas operacionales. 

La ubicación del puesto de trabajo puede ser indistintamente en Barcelona o Madrid. 

Requisitos mínimos

 

Titulación superior en matemáticas, estadística, ciencia e ingeniería de datos o similar.
Experiencia en análisis de modelos estadísticos/matemáticos avanzados: ajuste de distribuciones estadísticas, pruebas de hipótesis y validación estadística, análisis de colas y distribuciones extremas, simulaciones de Montecarlo, análisis de series temporales (modelos tradicionales y avanzados).
Conocimiento de Lenguajes de programación: R/Python (nivel avanzado).
Conocimiento de herramientas bases de datos: SQL/SAS (nivel avanzado).
Manejo de datos en Google Cloud Service (BigQuery, Vertex AI).
Conocimiento de modelos no paramétricos, especialmente para forecasting y text mining (NLP). 
Se valorará positivamente el cocimiento de Ingeniería de Datos y Optimización: pipeline para el procesamiento, transformación y consolidación de datos via SQL y DBT, Control de versiones con GitLab y ejecución eficiente de flujos SQL (DBT).
Competencias clave

 

Capacidad de análisis crítico y de síntesis; identificación de aspectos de mejora, y propuesta de iniciativas de solución.
Orientación a resultados y autonomía en el desarrollo de las tareas asignadas.
Flexibilidad, anticipación y adaptación a los cambios organizativos y del entorno.
Buena interlocución con otros departamentos.
Capacidad de relación y de trabajo en equipo. Requerirá interacción con distintas áreas de la organización.
Iniciativa propia y alta autonomía en el desarrollo de las tareas asignadas.
¿Qué ofrecemos?

 

Formar parte del Banco más innovador en Europa Occidental, según los premios The Innovators de la revista estadounidense Global Finance.
Programa de onboarding y acompañamiento personalizado para tu desarrollo profesional.  
Itinerario formativo individual con acceso a nuestra plataforma online, que ofrece un extenso catálogo de recursos de autoaprendizaje para fomentar tu crecimiento continuo.
Dispondrás de un seguro de salud completo totalmente gratuito para ti. Además, quedarás adherido al Plan de Pensiones, al que CaixaBank realizará aportaciones pensando ya en tu futuro.
Retribución flexible aplicada a transporte, formación, idiomas, guardería, entre otros.
Medidas de flexibilidad (trabajo en remoto, flexibilidad entrada)
Contamos con la certificación Top Employer, que nos reconoce como una de las mejores empresas para trabajar

  • Colaboración en el diseño e implementación del nuevo modelo de datos de pérdidas operacionales que permita la captura de nuevas fuentes de información y su tratamiento avanzado mediante:
    • Desarrollo de modelos predictivos mediante la utilización de técnicas de aprendizaje automático y modelos estadísticos para predecir eventos de riesgo y su impacto en la organización.
    • Desarrollo y evaluación de modelos híbridos, que combinan algoritmos de aprendizaje automático (ML) y técnicas de estadística avanzada, para la estimación de pérdidas operacionales, abordando desafíos como la escasez y heterogeneidad de datos, la optimización de distribuciones con colas pesadas y la estimación de eventos extremos.
  • Colaboración en la migración de procesos a Google Cloud (BigQuery, DBT, Vertex AI), diseñando pipelines escalables que aseguren la trazabilidad, reproducibilidad y eficiencia de los modelos predictivos, así como la automatización del reporting.
  • Colaboración en la preparación de datos para el reporting interno, principalmente en el Comité de Riesgo Operacional, así como involucración en informes y en comunicación con la Alta Dirección, Validación Interna, Auditoría y Supervisores.
  • Interrelación con los otros equipos de gestión especializados en pérdidas operacionales. 

La ubicación del puesto de trabajo puede ser indistintamente en Barcelona o Madrid. 

Requisitos mínimos

 

  • Titulación superior en matemáticas, estadística, ciencia e ingeniería de datos o similar.
  • Experiencia en análisis de modelos estadísticos/matemáticos avanzados: ajuste de distribuciones estadísticas, pruebas de hipótesis y validación estadística, análisis de colas y distribuciones extremas, simulaciones de Montecarlo, análisis de series temporales (modelos tradicionales y avanzados).
  • Conocimiento de Lenguajes de programación: R/Python (nivel avanzado).
  • Conocimiento de herramientas bases de datos: SQL/SAS (nivel avanzado).
  • Manejo de datos en Google Cloud Service (BigQuery, Vertex AI).
  • Conocimiento de modelos no paramétricos, especialmente para forecasting y text mining (NLP). 
  • Se valorará positivamente el cocimiento de Ingeniería de Datos y Optimización: pipeline para el procesamiento, transformación y consolidación de datos via SQL y DBT, Control de versiones con GitLab y ejecución eficiente de flujos SQL (DBT).
Competencias clave

 

  • Capacidad de análisis crítico y de síntesis; identificación de aspectos de mejora, y propuesta de iniciativas de solución.
  • Orientación a resultados y autonomía en el desarrollo de las tareas asignadas.
  • Flexibilidad, anticipación y adaptación a los cambios organizativos y del entorno.
  • Buena interlocución con otros departamentos.
  • Capacidad de relación y de trabajo en equipo. Requerirá interacción con distintas áreas de la organización.
  • Iniciativa propia y alta autonomía en el desarrollo de las tareas asignadas.
¿Qué ofrecemos?

 

  • Formar parte del Banco más innovador en Europa Occidental, según los premios The Innovators de la revista estadounidense Global Finance.
  • Programa de onboarding y acompañamiento personalizado para tu desarrollo profesional.  
  • Itinerario formativo individual con acceso a nuestra plataforma online, que ofrece un extenso catálogo de recursos de autoaprendizaje para fomentar tu crecimiento continuo.
  • Dispondrás de un seguro de salud completo totalmente gratuito para ti. Además, quedarás adherido al Plan de Pensiones, al que CaixaBank realizará aportaciones pensando ya en tu futuro.
  • Retribución flexible aplicada a transporte, formación, idiomas, guardería, entre otros.
  • Medidas de flexibilidad (trabajo en remoto, flexibilidad entrada)
  • Contamos con la certificación Top Employer, que nos reconoce como una de las mejores empresas para trabajar

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción de la empresa

MSX International es un líder global en servicios para el sector de automoción, presente en más de 80 países. Desde nuestra sede en Barcelona trabajamos con diferentes fabricantes internacionales para ofrecer soluciones innovadoras que impulsan la eficiencia, la calidad y el rendimiento de sus operaciones.

Si buscas un entorno dinámico, analítico, internacional y orientado a la mejora continua, este es tu sitio.

Descripción del empleo

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Tu misión será convertir grandes volúmenes de datos en insights claros y accionables que apoyen la toma de decisiones, la optimización de procesos y el rendimiento general del negocio.

Requisitos


Realizar análisis diarios, semanales y mensuales utilizando herramientas como Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Identificar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora
Preparar informes avanzados alineados con KPIs y requisitos del proyecto
Mantener, actualizar y asegurar la calidad de la documentación y reportes en herramientas como SharePoint y Office365
Identificar patrones en los datos y proponer acciones correctivas u oportunidades de optimización
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para convertir insights en acciones concretas
Garantizar el cumplimiento de estándares internos en informes y dashboards
Presentar análisis, conclusiones e información relevante a equipos internos y dirección
Asegurar la precisión, consistencia y calidad de los datos analizados
Proponer mejoras continuas en procesos, metodologías y flujos de análisis


Requisitos

Conocimientos y habilidades


Habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para trabajar con equipos diversos
Enfoque resolutivo para problemas complejos
Capacidad para gestionar prioridades y trabajar con plazos ajustados
Dominio de Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Conocimientos de sistemas de gestión de datos, minería de datos, procesos ETL y reporting automatizado
Competencias en análisis estadístico y modelos de rendimiento
Experiencia con herramientas de automatización (RPA) es un plus
Conocimiento de Microsoft Dynamics (deseable)


Formación


Grado en Ingeniería, Economía, Estadística, Matemáticas o formación equivalente


Experiencia


Experiencia previa demostrable como Analista de Datos


Idiomas


Español nativo
Inglés B2 (escrito y hablado)


Información adicional

Qué ofrecemos


Puesto permanente con contrato indefinido desde el primer día.
Política de teletrabajo de hasta el 50% de la jornada mensual tras el periodo de adaptación.
Formar parte de un gran equipo de profesionales en una compañía internacional consolidada.
Obtener una visión global del sector automoción, interactuando con clientes, proveedores, consultores y concesionarios.
Salario: 23.000 euros brutos anuales.
Acceso a MSX Bazaar, el portal de compras colectivas con grandes descuentos para empleados.
Posibilidad de adherirte al Plan de Retribución Flexible (seguro médico, tarjeta transporte, tarjeta restaurante, formación...).
23 días laborables de vacaciones al año según convenio y 3 días por año completo trabajado.
Incentivos adicionales por programas de recomendación interna, formadores internos, detección de nuevas oportunidades de negocio, etc.


¿Crees que eres la persona ideal para este puesto? ¡Queremos conocerte! Envíanos tu candidatura ahora.

MSX es un empleador que promueve la igualdad de oportunidades y alienta positivamente las solicitudes de candidatos debidamente cualificados y elegibles, independientemente de su sexo, raza, discapacidad, edad, orientación sexual, cambio de sexo, religión o creencias, estado civil, embarazo o maternidad. Como usuarios del programa de confianza en la discapacidad, garantizamos entrevistar a todos los candidatos discapacitados que cumplan los criterios mínimos para la vacante.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
We power people’s progress.

At Preply, we’re all about creating life-changing learning experiences. We help people discover the magic of the perfect tutor, craft a personalised learning journey, and stay motivated to keep growing. Our approach is human-led, tech-enabled - and it’s creating real impact.

We’ve just reached unicorn status with a $150M Series D, accelerating our vision to transform education through human-led, AI-enhanced learning. Today, 100,000+ tutors teach 90+ languages to learners in 180 countries - and we’re only getting started. As a category-defining company, we’re shaping what the future of learning looks like at global scale.

Every Preply lesson sparks change, fuels ambition, and drives progress that matters. Joining Preply means helping define the future of education at global scale, and building something that truly matters for millions of people, every day.

Meet the team!

At Preply, data is at the heart of every decision we make. We run hundreds of analysis to continually optimize our product, each with its own analytical and tracking challenges. The complexity of our subscription model, along with the unique dynamics of tutor-learner interactions, offers an exciting opportunity for those looking to make a real impact.

As a Staff Data Analyst , you will own the data strategy for a critical product domain (e.g., Growth, Retention, Marketplace) and act as the senior analytical authority in that space. You will partner with Directors and VPs across Business, Operations, Product, and Engineering to shape long-term strategy, influence investment decisions, and ensure our roadmap is grounded in rigorous, scalable measurement frameworks.

Our Data Team is dedicated to empowering top-quality decision-making. Do you want to know how? Visit our Tech Radar to learn about the technologies we use at Preply!

What you’ll be doing:


Develop and formalize a strategic understanding of the key value drivers of your domain, including user behavior, marketplace dynamics, pricing, and long-term unit economics — and translate these into actionable decision frameworks used by leadership.
Define the long-term measurement for your domain, identifying structural growth levers and step-change opportunities beyond incremental optimization.
Lead deep analytical investigations into customer behavior and retention dynamics, reframing ambiguous business questions into structured analyses that drive step-change improvements in long-term value.
Own impact modeling and scenario analysis for major product initiatives, enabling quarterly and annual planning decisions through robust forecasting, trade-off analysis, and risk assessment.
Own the definition and governance of key metrics within your domain, ensuring clarity, consistency, and alignment with company-level objectives.
Design the analytics programme in your domain and execute, and evaluate large-scale analysis to test new ideas and measure their effectiveness in driving business outcomes.
Act as a senior thought partner to business, operations, product, engineering, and data leaders, driving alignment across teams and elevating analytical standards across the organization.
Lead cross-domain analytical initiatives (e.g., forecasting, scenario modeling, performance optimization).


What you need to succeed:


Demonstrated track record of materially influencing business, operations or product strategy in complex environments, with clear examples of company-level impact.
Expertise in strategic analytics within a business pillar, ensuring consistent KPI definitions and analytical standards across teams. Resolving ambiguity across business and technical stakeholders, balances rapid reporting with deeper strategic research, and delivers decision-ready insights that clarify trade-offs and shape high-impact initiatives, even when data is imperfect or incomplete.
Strong understanding of subscription and marketplace economics, including LTV modeling, cohort dynamics, retention mechanics, pricing trade-offs, and long-term value optimization.
Strong technical proficiency (e.g., SQL, Python) with the ability to review and elevate the analytical work of others, set coding standards, and design scalable analytical workflows.
Curiosity, problem-solving and critical-thinking skills, as well as the ability to proactively identify and address challenges.
Ability to influence Directors and VP-level stakeholders, navigate conflicting incentives, and drive clarity in high-stakes strategic discussions.
Interest in the bigger picture, feeling excited to impact the product roadmap and strategy.
Experience mentoring and technically guiding senior data analyst, contributing to the overall growth and maturity of the data organization.


Nice to have:


Background in 2-sided marketplaces or digital businesses (B2C, B2B2C).
Experience in leading the application of advanced analytical approaches (e.g., forecasting, scenario modeling, performance optimization) to drive structural improvements across a business pillar. Aligning analytical outputs with unified KPI frameworks, evaluating opportunity costs and long-term impact, and synthesizing cross-functional insights into strategic recommendations.
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI).
Master's degree or PhD in a quantitative field.
Demonstrated experience developing senior ICs and contributing to raising the analytical bar across teams.


Why you’ll love it at Preply:


An open, collaborative, dynamic and diverse culture;
A generous monthly allowance for lessons on Preply.com, Learning & Development budget and time off for your self-development;
A competitive financial package with equity, leave allowance and health insurance;
Not in Barcelona? We offer an attractive relocation package to join us in our Preply Barcelona Hub
Access to free mental health support platforms;
Access to Gympass-partnered wellness and gym centers throughout Spain to promote and support well-being and physical health;
The opportunity to unlock the potential of learners and tutors through language learning and teaching in 175 countries (and counting!).


Our Principles


Care to change the world - We are passionate about our work and care deeply about its impact to be life changing.
We do it for learners - For both Preply and tutors, learners are why we do what we do. Every day we focus on empowering tutors to deliver an exceptional learning experience.
Keep perfecting - To create an outstanding customer experience, we focus on simplicity, smoothness, and enjoyment, continually perfecting it as every detail matters.
Now is the time - In a fast-paced world, it matters how quickly we act. Now is the time to make great things happen.
Disciplined execution - What makes us disciplined is the excellence in our execution. We set clear goals, focus on what matters, and utilize our resources efficiently.
Dive deep - We leverage business acumen and curiosity to investigate disparities between numbers and stories, unlocking meaningful insights to guide our decisions.
Growth mindset - We proactively seek growth opportunities and believe today's best performance becomes tomorrow's starting point. We humbly embrace feedback and learn from setbacks.
Raise the bar - We raise our performance standards continuously, alongside each new hire and promotion. We build diverse and high-performing teams that can make a real difference.
Challenge, disagree and commit - We value open and candid communication, even when we don’t fully agree. We speak our minds, challenge when necessary, and fully commit to decisions once made.
One Preply - We prioritize collaboration, inclusion, and the success of our team over personal ambitions. Together, we support and celebrate each other's progress.


Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

Preply.com is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people of diverse backgrounds can thrive. We believe that the presence of different opinions and viewpoints is a key ingredient for our success as a multicultural Ed-Tech company. That means that Preply will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age or veteran status.

Title: Financial Data Analyst (con IA)
Description:
Estamos buscando un perfil enfocado en análisis financiero y elaboración de informes, para incorporarse al área de Assurance (auditoría, forensic y proyectos especiales).


El candidato trabajará junto a un equipo de auditores senior, por lo que no se requiere un perfil altamente especializado en contabilidad avanzada, pero sí una base sólida en finanzas y capacidad de aprendizaje rápido.


Responsabilidades principales

Elaboración de informes financieros, incluyendo informes periciales y especiales

Participación en proyectos de due diligence y auditoría

Análisis de estados financieros consolidados

Redacción de informes con alta calidad narrativa y claridad analítica

Apoyo al equipo de auditoría en el análisis de información financiera compleja

Identificación de riesgos, desviaciones y puntos críticos en la información financiera




Requisitos


Formación en Economía, ADE o similar

Experiencia mínima de 2 años en contabilidad, auditoría o despacho financiero

Capacidad analítica y atención al detalle

Mentalidad de aprendizaje y crecimiento profesional

Interés por desarrollarse en el área de auditoría y análisis financiero

Experiencia o conocimientos en Data o herramientas de IA aplicada a análisis financiero

Interés por la tecnología (sin necesidad de programar)

Perfil ideal


Buscamos una persona con:


Alta capacidad analítica y estructurada

Motivación por aprender y desarrollarse en auditoría

Capacidad para investigar, interpretar datos y extraer conclusiones

Buen nivel de redacción y comunicación escrita

Perfil joven, flexible y con ambición de crecimiento

Enfoque práctico, resolutivo y orientado a resultados




Ventajas

Condiciones

Horario: 9:00 – 18:00 (viernes hasta 14:00)

Teletrabajo: 2 días por semana

Ubicación: Barcelona

Salario: 30000€-33000€

Title: Revenue Management Analyst
Description:
Overview

Revenue Management Analyst

Design and implement the Pack, Price and Promotion Strategy across the different channels, with the objective of maximizing PepsiCo’s Net Revenue & Profitability.

This role sits at the intersection of consumer & commercial strategy, and financial performance, ensuring the right value propositions reach the right consumers, through the right channels, at the right price and in the right formats, while balancing profitability, competitiveness, and customer needs.

This role must have close collaboration with Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Category teams to drive sustainable growth through data-driven decision-making.

Responsibilities

Main Accountabilities:


Design and recommend Pack & Price strategies across brands and different sub-channels, grounded in insights and performance analysis
Lead Promotional strategy definition and implementation. Define the annual promo calendar, based on shopper insights to maximize performance. Perform periodic analysis to understand results & tailor the strategy.
Drive mix development initiatives to maximize Net Revenue Realization (NRR), acting as a category-wide owner of mix levers across channels and departments.
Own PPA initiatives, working together with sales, marketing & commercialization, ensuring market-fit and strategic relevance.
Lead the AOP strategy & algorithm, aligning short- and long-term initiatives to deliver volume, revenue, and profitability targets.
Create x-channel commercial policy, and define list prices for all SKUs, participating in the innovation gates.
Partner with Sales to co-create commercially sound proposals, including multipacks , bonuspacks or I&Os in order to boost volumes.
Support the implementation & adoption RGM Digital tools (e.g. elasticity, ROI, Uplift models…)
Anticipate proactively the market changes, for analysis and decision making (VAT or Tax impacts, legislations, sustainability goals..etc)
Monitor and track implemented initiatives in order to obtain learnings & insights to optimize and define future strategies.
Ensure cross-functional alignment across Sales, Trade Marketing, Insights, Finance, Marketing, Category Management, and Commercialization.


Qualifications

Experience required


At least 7 years experience in Revenue Management, Finance Planning, Insights, Commercial roles or similar.
Experience in the use of internal & external data bases (Nielsen, SAP,…)
Knowledge of RGM & finance KPIs (NR, MC, Elasticity, ROI..) preferred
Proactivity, ability to work in a cross functional environment and with collaboration spirit
Strong Microsoft Office skills (Excel and ppt)
Fluent in Spanish and English (advanced level)


Competencies Required


Good story telling and communication skills
Very strong analytical skills & high business knowledge as a broad perspective is needed to identify channel opportunities
Strategic thinking. Ability to see connection in data, anticipate potential risks/opportunities, and optimize & simplify processes.
Self-driven, able to work independently and take initiative
Team player & Proactive collaboration
Resilience and adaptability to change
Capacity to work under pressure and tight deadlines
Experience in stakeholder management


Why PepsiCo Iberia?

PepsiCo Iberia, part of the Western Europe Operating Unit, is home to a team of nearly 3,000 talented employees across our Barcelona, Madrid, Vitoria and Lisbon headquarters, and our production sites in Spain and Portugal.

With iconic brands like Pepsi, 7UP, Alvalle, Lay’s or Doritos, we continue to shape the food and beverage landscape as one of the region’s leading companies.

At PepsiCo, you’re not just joining a business - you’re joining a culture that champions innovation, encourages bold thinking and empowers every individual to make an impact.

Title: Aviation Senior Data Analyst - Data, Insights & AI | Hybrid | Barcelona / Madrid | Advance English
Description:
About us

DATWIN is a team of senior data professionals dedicated to delivering high-impact, high-quality solutions by using cutting-edge technologies and promoting a collaborative approach. Our mission is to foster a data-driven culture and help organisations make strategic decisions more effectively.




We place strong emphasis on building honest and trusted relationships with our clients, ensuring that every delivery creates value from day one. With a focus on transparency and reliability, at DATWIN we aim to help companies grow through the power of data.




Role Description

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst - Data & Insights with high interest in AI to join a high-impact transformation project for one of the world’s leading aviation groups.




This role is focused on turning complex data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic decision-making across the organisation. You will work at the intersection of advanced analytics, business performance, and emerging AI capabilities, contributing to initiatives related to commercial performance, operational analytics, executive reporting, and the evolution of analytics platforms.




This is an ideal opportunity for someone with very strong analytical expertise, business acumen, technological curiosity, and a proactive mindset to explore how AI can improve productivity, insight generation, and data adoption within a complex international organisation.




What you’ll do

Deliver high-quality analytical insights to support decision-making across commercial, operational, and finance domains
Translate complex data into clear, structured outputs for senior stakeholders
Support executive-level reporting and business performance tracking
Work closely with business teams to understand needs, define KPIs, and build performance frameworks
Maintain, improve, and evolve existing reporting solutions, including Power BI dashboards
Contribute to the design and evolution of data products and self-service analytics solutions
Collaborate with data engineering and platform teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and usability
Explore AI-driven tools and approaches to improve productivity, insight generation, and data discoverability
Contribute to initiatives that improve data accessibility, adoption, and usage across the organisation




What we’re looking for

6+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or similar roles
Strong experience working with large datasets and analytical tools
Strong SQL skills
Experience with Python for analysis, automation, or data processing
Experience with Power BI or similar BI and reporting tools
Proven ability to turn analysis into actionable business insights
Good understanding of business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational KPIs, or commercial metrics
Experience working with stakeholders at different levels, including senior management
Exposure to AI tools, GenAI, ML tools, or analytics copilots
Curiosity and willingness to experiment with new technologies, especially in AI
Experience in aviation, travel, transport, or other complex data-rich industries will be highly valued
Location: Madrid / Barcelona
Hybrid working model
Advance English level or Native




What Will Make You a Great Fit

You enjoy transforming complex data into clear business messages
You think in terms of impact, not just dashboards or reports
You have the judgement to define relevant KPIs and challenge weak metrics
You are comfortable working with senior stakeholders
You enjoy exploring new AI tools applied to analytics
You can work effectively in ambiguous and transformation-driven environments
You combine attention to detail with business understanding
You communicate in a clear, structured, and decision-oriented way
You are comfortable working in a fast-paced, transformation-driven environment




How We Work

We look for autonomous, structured, and responsible people
We value quality over quantity
We expect analytical judgement and the ability to challenge requirements
We prefer simple, clear, and actionable solutions
We work with a strong focus on real business impact
We encourage collaboration across business, data, and technology teams




What We Offer

Senior, high-performing team without unnecessary hierarchy
Permanent contract
Hybrid working model
Continuous learning programme
Technical certifications
Team building events throughout the year
An environment where clarity, honesty, and real impact matter




If you are looking to:

Work on analytics with real business impact
Contribute to transformation projects within the aviation / travel sector
Explore how AI can improve the way organisations generate insights
Collaborate with a senior, demanding, and honest team




We want to meet you.

💪💪💪 Join Us 💪💪💪

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Level Up Your Career with Zynga!

At Zynga, we bring people together through the power of play. As a global leader in interactive entertainment and a proud label of Take-Two Interactive, our games have been downloaded over 6 billion times—connecting players in 175+ countries through fun, strategy, and a little friendly competition.

From thrilling casino spins to epic strategy battles, mind-bending puzzles, and social word challenges, our diverse game portfolio has something for everyone. Fan-favorites and latest hits include FarmVille™, Words With Friends™, Zynga Poker™, Game of Thrones Slots Casino™, Wizard of Oz Slots™, Hit it Rich! Slots™, Wonka Slots™, Top Eleven™, Toon Blast™, Empires & Puzzles™, Merge Dragons!™, CSR Racing™, Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells™, Match Factory™, and Color Block Jam™—plus many more!

Founded in 2007 and headquartered in California, our teams span North America, Europe, and Asia, working together to craft unforgettable gaming experiences. Whether you're spinning, strategizing, matching, or competing, Zynga is where fun meets innovation—and where you can take your career to the next level.

Join us and be part of the play!

Position Overview

We are looking to welcome yet another brilliant Senior Marketing Data Analyst into our Marketing Analytics team.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you’ll be responsible for designing and driving high-impact analytics solutions, from developing advanced statistical frameworks to evaluate marketing performance, to building data pipelines that integrate third-party sources, to creating interactive tools that empower partner teams. These are just a few examples of the initiatives you’ll help lead and deliver end-to-end, playing a key role in shaping the technical direction of our analytics efforts. The demands on this team are constantly evolving with the needs of Socialpoint’s and Zynga’s dynamic marketing teams, so it is important that you be able to adapt and utilize your time efficiently.

What You'll Do


Partner closely with marketing and cross-functional teams to identify growth opportunities, improve efficiency, and shape strategic initiatives.
Lead analytical deep dives to surface insights and translate them into actionable recommendations for senior stakeholders.
Visualize and communicate analytical findings clearly, enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Apply statistical methodologies to evaluate performance and account for uncertainties in major initiatives.
Proactively explore and prototype opportunities using AI-driven automation to streamline investigations and improve decision-making.


What You Bring


Demonstrated experience with some or all of the following: predictive modeling, statistics, experimental design, optimization, data visualization
Understanding of marketing metrics and KPIs, with experience in campaign optimization, Lifetime Value (LTV) modeling, and incrementality analysis

At Zynga, we bring people together through the power of play. Slots™, Wonka Slots™, Top Eleven™, Toon Blast™, Empires & Puzzles™, Merge Dragons!™, CSR Racing™, Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells™, Match Factory™, and Color Block Jam™—plus many more!

Founded in 2007 and headquartered in California, our teams span North America, Europe, and Asia, working together to craft unforgettable gaming experiences. Whether you're spinning, strategizing, matching, or competing, Zynga is where fun meets innovation—and where you can take your career to the next level.

Join us and be part of the play!

Position Overview

We are looking to welcome yet another brilliant Senior Marketing Data Analyst into our Marketing Analytics team.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you’ll be responsible for designing and driving high-impact analytics solutions, from developing advanced statistical frameworks to evaluate marketing performance, to building data pipelines that integrate third-party sources, to creating interactive tools that empower partner teams. These are just a few examples of the initiatives you’ll help lead and deliver end-to-end, playing a key role in shaping the technical direction of our analytics efforts. The demands on this team are constantly evolving with the needs of Socialpoint’s and Zynga’s dynamic marketing teams, so it is important that you be able to adapt and utilize your time efficiently.

What You'll Do

  • Partner closely with marketing and cross-functional teams to identify growth opportunities, improve efficiency, and shape strategic initiatives.
  • Lead analytical deep dives to surface insights and translate them into actionable recommendations for senior stakeholders.
  • Visualize and communicate analytical findings clearly, enabling data-driven decision-making across the organization.
  • Apply statistical methodologies to evaluate performance and account for uncertainties in major initiatives.
  • Proactively explore and prototype opportunities using AI-driven automation to streamline investigations and improve decision-making.

What You Bring
