Title: Analyst Trainee para a Unidade de Análise de Operações
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander Portugal está à procura de um Analyst Trainee para a Unidade de Análise de Operações motivado(a), com forte curiosidade intelectual e vontade de aprender, para integrar a equipa de Riscos de Crédito no Porto.

Nesta função, terás a oportunidade de trabalhar lado a lado com uma equipa experiente e multidisciplinar, desenvolvendo competências técnicas e analíticas essenciais na avaliação de risco de crédito em operações de financiamento de pequenos negócios ou empresas.

A área de riscos de Crédito é a unidade especializada na análise de operações de crédito propostas pelos nossos clientes em todos os segmentos de clientes do banco (Particulares, empresas e Grandes Empresas

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Analisar as propostas em fase de admissão que sejam remetidas pelo motor de decisão automático e que não sejam decididas por esse sistema para todos os produtos de retalho, para o segmento de Clientes Particulares, nomeadamente: crédito habitação, crédito ao consumo e cartões de crédito, avaliando a solvabilidade conforme as regras macroprudenciais e perfil dos clientes, garantindo o cumprimento dos critérios de avaliação de risco do Santander;
Cumprir com os níveis de serviço (SLA) estabelecidos, assegurando a resposta adequada e atempada no processo de admissão de risco de crédito particulares;
Analisar pedidos de alterações aos contratos em curso
Analisar pedidos de renegociação das condições contratuais motivadas por eventuais incapacidades de pagamento do crédito (Clientes PARI e PERSI), entre outros;
Decidir as operações analisadas dentro dos poderes de crédito delegados ou emitir parecer com apresentação e submissão das propostas a comissão de crédito;
Propor melhorias / fine tuning de regras de admissão que possam ser industrializadas e incorporadas no motor de decisão, com base na experiência da atividade gerida, que contribuam para o aumento da eficiência do sistema de admissão.


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

Experiência Profissional


Experiência prévia em estágio (preferencialmente numa Big4) é valorizada, mas não obrigatória — o mais importante é a motivação e vontade de aprender.


Formação Académica


Formação académica concluída ou em fase de conclusão em Economia, Gestão, Finanças, Contabilidade, ou áreas similares.


Idiomas


Domínio de inglês.
Conhecimento de espanhol (valorizado).


Hard Skills


Bons conhecimentos de Microsoft Office, nomeadamente Excel avançado e Powerpoint (conhecimentos de Power BI são valorizados);


Soft Skills


Sentido de responsabilidade e compromisso com a qualidade do trabalho.
Atenção ao detalhe e foco no rigor.
Vontade genuína de aprender, crescer e assumir novos desafios.
Capacidade analítica e curiosidade intelectual.
Capacidade de gerir pressão e de manter a qualidade do trabalho em momentos de maior exigência.
Boa organização, capacidade de gerir prioridades.
Gosto por trabalhar em equipa e comunicar de forma clara.
Interesse por análise empresas, mercados financeiros e risco.


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Title: Banking Sector | Data Analyst
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description


Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret data to generate actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions
Build, maintain, and monitor data pipelines, ensuring reliable extraction, processing, and loading of data
Work closely with end-users and cross-functional teams to ensure data accessibility, usability, and stability
Transform complex information into clear, engaging, and valuable deliverables for business stakeholders
Assess business changes and requirements, evaluating their impact and associated costs
Optimize code, queries, and data processes to improve efficiency and performance
Develop and maintain Airflow pipelines for data extraction, processing, and loading
Optimize Python scripts, SQL queries, and overall data workflows
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities to enhance user experience and anticipate needs
Deliver insights and visual representations that support daily decision-making
Understand business requirements and translate them into precise data solutions



Qualifications


Proven background in data analysis, with experience transforming raw data into actionable insights
Strong expertise in designing and maintaining Airflow pipelines for ETL processes
Proficiency in Python and SQL, with experience in Oracle Database environments and API development
Familiarity with Data Warehouses and large-scale data ecosystems
Experience with Git version control and working in Agile environments
Ability to optimize code, identify data patterns, and produce clear, engaging data outputs
Proficient in English



Additional Information

Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: Analista de Dados (M/F)
HTML:
A GreenVenture é uma empresa privada de investimento e gestão de ativos especializada na identificação, aquisição, desenvolvimento e implementação de projetos de energia renovável.


Acreditamos no poder da ação coletiva e da inovação para criar um futuro sustentável. Procuramos reforçar a nossa estrutura com um Analista de dados (m/f).


Missão:

Para suportar o crescimento das nossas áreas de produção e comercialização de energia, procuramos profissional para desenvolvimento de modelos matemáticos aplicados a mercados de eletricidade;


Responsabilidades:

  • Desenvolvimento e manutenção de modelos de previsão multi-variável e multi-horizonte (produção solar e eólica, consumo, preço, desvios);
  • Desenvolver dashboards de monitorização de desempenho e relatórios analíticos (KPIs de precisão, eficiência e retorno);
  • Implementar rotinas de data cleaning, atualização automática de datasets e version control de modelos;
  • Sistemas de decisão em tempo real para mercados grossistas e mercados de serviços de sistema;
  • Colaborar com a equipa técnica e de gestão na interpretação dos resultados preditivos e na sua integração nas decisões de trading;
  • Validação, monitorização e backtesting de modelos em ambiente operacional;
  • Colaboração com a equipa de operação na definição de requisitos e avaliação de resultados.


Perfil:

  • Formação superior em Matemática (pura ou aplicada), Física ou Engenharia Física. Áreas afins com sólida base quantitativa (Estatística, Data Science) poderão ser consideradas;
  • Domínio sólido de Python (numpy, pandas); familiaridade com bibliotecas de ML e otimização (scikit-learn, PyTorch, Pyomo, OR-Tools) constitui uma mais-valia;
  • Conforto com séries temporais, modelação probabilística, otimização sob restrições e teoria da decisão;
  • Capacidade de trabalho com dados incompletos, multi-fonte e em diferentes granularidades temporais;
  • Pensamento crítico e rigor analítico.


Oferta:

  • Integração numa empresa sólida e em crescimento;
  • Equipa com estrutura profissional e dinâmica;
  • Remuneração em função da experiência demonstrada.

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.

Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid

What Will You Do As Data Analyst


Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.


Key Qualifications

Must have:


Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.


Nice Have


Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.


Personal Skills


Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.


What ISS Offers You

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:


Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.


Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.

ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.

ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
At LUXCLUSIF, we are dedicated to redefining the luxury paradigm by offering premium pre-owned fashion to a global audience. As a pioneer in the global luxury resale market, we utilize advanced data-driven solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of high-end pre-owned items. With operations spread across the globe and strategic warehouses in key locations, we ensure a seamless supply of luxury goods. Our comprehensive services cover everything from item curation, quality control, operations, logistics to technology integrations and sales, delivering an unmatched resale experience for brands, retailers, and individual customers alike.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
As a Senior Data Analyst, you will join our Product Analytics team to champion data best practices and strengthen our technical foundations. You will deliver impactful insights regarding our tech products while playing a pivotal role in evolving our self-service data environment. By partnering closely with our Data Engineering team and power users across verticals, you will centralize business logic, optimize our data models, and ensure our analytics products remain performant, scalable, and governed.

What You'll Do


Centralize Business Logic: Identify decentralized logic (such as complex cost calculations currently living inside Power BI semantic models) and push it upstream into centralized, well-documented data models
Empower Self-Service: Provide our vertical power users with a clean, highly performant, and governed data foundation so they can confidently build the reports they need independently
Champion Data Governance: Drive data governance efforts across our growing self-service ecosystem, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across the company
Mentor & Elevate: Act as technical anchor for our team, mentoring on SQL optimization, dimensional modeling, and BI best practices
Deliver Product Analytics: Maintain and evolve core company reports while providing deep-dive analytics on our tech products to support strategic business decisions





Who You Are


Experienced Professional: You have 5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Analytics Engineering, or Business Intelligence, with a strong track record of improving data infrastructure and performance
Tech Savvy: You possess expert-level SQL skills and extensive experience with Power BI (including DAX optimization and semantic models) and cloud data platforms like Azure (particularly Databricks). Ideally, you also bring hands-on experience with dbt and Google BigQuery as we plan to evolve our data stack
Architectural Thinker: You can easily identify bottlenecks in data pipelines or decentralized logic, and you take the initiative to design and implement efficient, scalable solutions
Collaborative Mentor: You are a team player who enjoys collaborating closely with data team members and stakeholders
Effective Communicator: You excel at translating complex technical concepts and governance standards to non-technical stakeholders and power users





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools

At LUXCLUSIF, we are dedicated to redefining the luxury paradigm by offering premium pre-owned fashion to a global audience. As a pioneer in the global luxury resale market, we utilize advanced data-driven solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of high-end pre-owned items. With operations spread across the globe and strategic warehouses in key locations, we ensure a seamless supply of luxury goods. Our comprehensive services cover everything from item curation, quality control, operations, logistics to technology integrations and sales, delivering an unmatched resale experience for brands, retailers, and individual customers alike.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
As a Senior Data Analyst, you will join our Product Analytics team to champion data best practices and strengthen our technical foundations. You will deliver impactful insights regarding our tech products while playing a pivotal role in evolving our self-service data environment. By partnering closely with our Data Engineering team and power users across verticals, you will centralize business logic, optimize our data models, and ensure our analytics products remain performant, scalable, and governed.

What You'll Do

  • Centralize Business Logic: Identify decentralized logic (such as complex cost calculations currently living inside Power BI semantic models) and push it upstream into centralized, well-documented data models
  • Empower Self-Service: Provide our vertical power users with a clean, highly performant, and governed data foundation so they can confidently build the reports they need independently
  • Champion Data Governance: Drive data governance efforts across our growing self-service ecosystem, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across the company
  • Mentor & Elevate: Act as technical anchor for our team, mentoring on SQL optimization, dimensional modeling, and BI best practices
  • Deliver Product Analytics: Maintain and evolve core company reports while providing deep-dive analytics on our tech products to support strategic business decisions




Who You Are

  • Experienced Professional: You have 5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Analytics Engineering, or Business Intelligence, with a strong track record of improving data infrastructure and performance
  • Tech Savvy: You possess expert-level SQL skills and extensive experience with Power BI (including DAX optimization and semantic models) and cloud data platforms like Azure (particularly Databricks). Ideally, you also bring hands-on experience with dbt and Google BigQuery as we plan to evolve our data stack
  • Architectural Thinker: You can easily identify bottlenecks in data pipelines or decentralized logic, and you take the initiative to design and implement efficient, scalable solutions
  • Collaborative Mentor: You are a team player who enjoys collaborating closely with data team members and stakeholders
  • Effective Communicator: You excel at translating complex technical concepts and governance standards to non-technical stakeholders and power users




Rewards & Benefits

  • Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools

Title: Data Analyst – Dataiku
Description:
🌍 About Keyrus

Keyrus is a global consulting and technology company focused on architecting intelligence, helping organisations transform data, digital capabilities, and AI into sustainable business value.

Founded in 1996, Keyrus operates in 28+ countries across 5 continents, with more than 3,300 people worldwide. Our strength comes from combining deep expertise in Data & Analytics, AI, Digital, and Management Consulting with a strong understanding of business realities.

Data is never the goal in itself.

We use data to shape understanding, design meaningful experiences, and enable better, real-life decisions.

At Keyrus, we also believe companies have a responsibility beyond performance. Through our Foundation and ESG initiatives, we actively contribute to sustainability, inclusion, and positive societal impact.

#OneTeamOneKeyrus

🎯 The Role

As a Data Analyst, you will work at the intersection of data, technology, and business, helping transform complex datasets into actionable insights that support business, compliance, and operational decision-making.

You will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams in an international environment and contribute to data-driven initiatives that improve reporting, controls, and business performance.

📍 Job location: Porto or Lisbon (Hybrid)

🕒 Contract type: Employee

🗓 Target start date: ASAP

⏰ Working hours: Full-time (40h/week)

💵 Compensation: €35,000 – €45,000/year

Note: All applications (CVs) must be submitted in English.

💥 Your Impact

In This Role, You Will


Gather and analyse business requirements from stakeholders.
Translate business needs into data workflows and reporting solutions.
Develop Dataiku workflows and reconciliation processes.
Create impactful Power BI dashboards and visualizations.
Monitor data quality and perform validation activities.
Generate actionable insights to support decision-making.
Support compliance, controls, and operational reporting initiatives.


⚡ What Makes This Role Challenging


You will work with complex and highly regulated datasets.
Business requirements often evolve and require adaptable solutions.
Data quality and reporting accuracy are critical.
You will collaborate with both business and technical stakeholders.


👤 What We’re Looking For

Must-haves


Bachelor's or Master's Degree in a relevant field.
3+ years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or Analytics.
Dataiku DSS experience.
Strong Power BI skills.
Advanced SQL knowledge.
Experience with data validation, reconciliation, and reporting.
Strong analytical and communication skills.
Fluency in English.
Must be a Portuguese or European citizen, or hold a valid work permit for Portugal.


Nice-to-haves


Python.
Banking or Financial Services experience.
Data Governance exposure.
Regulatory reporting knowledge.
French language skills.
Alteryx or similar workflow automation tools.


💰 How Our Salary Ranges Work

At Keyrus, salary ranges reflect different levels of mastery and impact within the same role — not different job titles.


Bottom of the range You meet the core requirements and will need ramp-up time and support.
Middle of the range You are fully autonomous from Day 1 and deliver consistently.
Top of the range You are a reference for the role, mentor others, and raise the bar for the team.


Final offers are based on experience, autonomy, scope, and market context, and are discussed transparently during the process.

🎁 What We Offer At Keyrus Portugal


Competitive salary aligned with your experience and the data market
Meal allowance: €10.20/day
Flexible benefits plan
Private medical insurance
22 days of annual leave, increasing every 3 years (up to 25 days)
Continuous learning via KLX – Keyrus Learning Experience
A collaborative, international, and human-centred work environment


💙 Why Keyrus?

Joining Keyrus Means Joining


A market leader in Data Intelligence
A company where people, trust, and diversity are core values
An environment that values ownership, flexibility, and innovation
A place where different backgrounds and perspectives are not just welcomed = they are essential


We believe diversity drives better thinking, stronger teams, and better outcomes.

Everyone belongs at Keyrus.

At Keyrus, all stages of our recruitment process are conducted and evaluated by human recruiters and interviewers.

To support accuracy and efficiency, AI may occasionally be used internally by our team exclusively for note-taking purposes during interviews. AI is never used to make hiring decisions or evaluate candidates.

🔒 To ensure fairness, authenticity, and the protection of confidential and proprietary information, the use of AI tools by candidates during the recruitment process is strictly prohibited.

Our commitment to responsible AI practices ensures that hiring decisions are based solely on each candidate’s own skills, experience, judgment, and expertise.

⚠️ Any use of AI assistance during the interview process may result in immediate disqualification from the recruitment process.

♿ Equal Opportunity Statement

We are committed to building an inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all backgrounds, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Title: Internship Data Analyst (m./f./div.)
Description:
Company Description

The Keenfinity Group delivers professional communication and security solutions that connect and protect people and assets. Following its carve-out from the Bosch Group in mid-2025, it operates as independent company within the portfolio of European investment firm Triton. The Group’s four Businesses include Audio delivering professional communication products of the globally renowned brands Bosch, Electro-Voice, Dynacord, RTS and Telex, IQSIGHT Video Systems, Radionix Intrusion & Access, and KEENFINITY Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS). In fiscal year 2025, the group generated revenues of over €1 billion and employed approximately 4,000 people across more than 40 countries.

Job Description

SCOPE OF ROLE & MAIN KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


Collect and combine data from multiple data sources and applications
Support in data cleansing activities
Extract, analyze and provide insights from data to the businesses
Calculate data metrics and KPIs to track data completeness and quality
Define preventive and corrective measures to improve data quality
Perform statistical analysis and create predictive models to automate decision making
Build reports and dashboards to ensure transparency


Qualifications


Academic Degree in management, business informatics, engineering or similar
Data analysis tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
SQL, Python or R programming skills
Knowledge of different file types and how to read them (XML, CSV, JSON, XLSX, TXT, etc.)
Agile methodologies, namely Scrum and Kanban
Project management and documentation tools (Jira, Confluence, or similar)
Excellent communication skills (oral and written) in English
Innovative with continuous improvement mindset
Critical and structured thinking
Meticulous with high attention to detail
Curious about new technologies and strange data patterns


Additional Information

Keenfinity benefits includes:

⚖️ Flexible work conditions

🔀 Hybrid work system

🍽️ Canteen

🅿️ Free parking lot

🏋️ Sports and health related activities (gym)

📚 Training opportunities (i.e., technical training, foreign languages training) & certifications

📈 Opportunities for career progression and continuous professional development

🌐 Exchange with colleagues around the world

💲 Access to great discounts in partnerships and products

🌍All our positions are open to people with disability





At Keenfinity we don’t just build innovative solutions — we shape a smarter, more connected world through technology.

We value different backgrounds, ideas, and experiences and we’re committed to growing, learning, and celebrating success as one team. Everyone is welcome here — we foster an environment where everyone is respected, valued, and encouraged to be their authentic self.

Keenfinity is an equal opportunity employer, offering equal opportunities for all. We welcome applications from people with disabilities and can offer support, if needed. When everyone has a chance to contribute, we all do better.

Title: People Data Analyst
Description:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

PEOPLE TEAM

The People team plays a pivotal role in supporting the business by strategically overseeing the complete employee journey. From drawing in top talent and fostering a high-performance culture and career development, they are instrumental in creating a positive and inclusive workplace environment and managing our workspaces.

PORTO

Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

ROLE

As part of the People Data & Insights team, you will be a key player in our mission to shape our organizational strategy and empower the business with data-driven insights. We are the analytical and strategic engine that enables the People Team and senior leadership to make smarter, more strategic decisions about our organization and our people. This role will provide you with a deep understanding of the intersection of People Analytics, business strategy, organizational transformation, and process automation. You will be at the center of critical initiatives that drive our long-term vision and operational efficiency.

What You'll Do


Provide Strategic Analysis: Be passionate about optimization and critical thinking. You will analyze complex headcount data and people-related financial numbers, providing key insights for forecasting, budgeting, and identifying efficiency opportunities
Collaborate Across the Business: Liaise with multiple internal and external stakeholders globally, ensuring strategic analysis and project updates are channelled effectively through the business
Champion Process Automation: Identify and analyze existing People processes, designing clean, automated, and scalable solutions, using the most recent tech capabilities (AI-driven) that flow smoothly and unlock more time for high-value work
Transform Data into Actionable Insights: Developing dashboards and reports ensuring their correct communication to all involved stakeholders. You will transform complex data into clear, actionable insights for leadership
Leverage Our HR Tech Stack: Maximize the potential of our core HRIS, Workday, by building and enhancing its native dashboards and reports. Your focus will be on creating intuitive, self-service analytics tools that empower our stakeholders with on-demand data to drive their decisions
Drive Strategic & Transformational Projects: Be a key player in multi-disciplinary, end-to-end project teams to address new business needs, driving continuous improvement and strategic change initiatives related to our people and processes





Who You Are


Degree in Economics, Management, Mathematics, Engineering or other relevant quantitative degree
2-3 years of professional experience mandatory
Fluent English is mandatory
Proficiency in Excel and Google Sheets is mandatory
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI is a strong plus
Familiarity with BigQuery is a plus
Familiarity with AI solutions is a plus
Proven experience in a similar role blending data analysis, project management, and strategic thinking
Strong numerical reasoning, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Organized and detail-oriented with a proactive approach to managing projects end-to-end





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities and free access to Udemy
Flexible benefits program





EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT



Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.





SCAM DISCLAIMER



It has come to our attention that there may be fraudulent activities involving individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent Farfetch in order to attract candidates to a SCAM. Please be aware that Farfetch does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Additionally, Farfetch will never ask candidates for any form of payment during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.




Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid







What will you do as Data Analyst:

Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.




Key qualifications:

Must have:

Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.




Nice have:

Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.




Personal skills:

Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.




What ISS Offers You:

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:

Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.




Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst
Description:
HBK is a leading organization in the testing and measurement industry that is on a journey to ensure it leverages data-driven insights to make strategic business decisions. We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in enhancing our data analytics and reporting capabilities. The team is currently rebuilding our data landscape, and this is a real opportunity to drive change within the business area.

Job Summary:

As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you will play a key role in gathering, analyzing, and transforming data into actionable insights that drive informed decision-making at HBK. Drawing upon your extensive expertise in Business Intelligence, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver advanced data solutions and enhance operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities:


Data Strategy: Develop and execute a comprehensive data strategy that leverages our data assets to meet the organization’s analytical needs and objectives.
Advanced Analytics: Apply advanced statistical and predictive analytics techniques to uncover trends, patterns, and insights in data, enabling more informed decision-making.
Reporting Excellence: Lead the design and maintenance of sophisticated, interactive reports and dashboards using modern business intelligence and data visualization platforms (SAP Analytics Cloud, Power BI, or similar tools). Create highly visual and intuitive data visualizations for use by business stakeholders.
Data Integration and Governance: Oversee the integration of data from diverse sources, ensuring data quality, consistency, and adherence to data governance policies. Collaborate with IT and data engineering teams to optimize data pipelines.
Performance Optimization: Continuously enhance the performance of our systems, including optimizing data processing and reporting capabilities.
Business Requirements Analysis: Work closely with business leaders to understand their analytical needs and translate these requirements into actionable data solutions.
Data Modeling Expertise: Design and implement complex data models to facilitate advanced reporting and analytics, encompassing both logical and physical data models.
Data Security and Compliance: Ensure strict data security and compliance with internal policies and industry regulations throughout all data handling and reporting activities.
Mentorship and Training: Provide guidance and training to other analysts and end-users, fostering self-service analytics adoption.


Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field (Master's degree preferred).
A minimum of 3 years of experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst.
In-depth expertise in data warehousing, ETL processes, data modeling, and advanced analytics techniques.
Proficiency in SQL and familiarity with data visualization tools.
Exceptional problem-solving skills and analytical thinking.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Proven ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
Knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in business intelligence and data analytics.


Additional Information:


Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.
Opportunities for career growth and professional development within our organization.
A collaborative and innovative work environment equipped with state-of-the-art data analytics tools.


If you are a seasoned professional with a passion for driving advanced data-driven decision-making, we invite you to apply and become a pivotal part of our team at HBK. Join us in shaping the future of data analytics for our business's success.

We Offer

Freedom with responsibility is the framework for HBK’s employees. This allows for a good balance between work and family life and for constant development of professional and personal skills in an international and enjoyable working environment.

One Company – HBK

On 1 January 2019, Brüel & Kjær and HBM merged their activities into a new company called HBK (Hottinger Brüel & Kjær). HBK is a subsidiary of Spectris Limited, and employees 3,500 employees worldwide and production facilities in Denmark, Germany, the UK, the USA, China and Portugal and presence in 80 countries, HBK is a leading player in the test and measurement area. For further info please visit www.hbkworld.com.

Title: Junior Consultant
Description:
Company Description Lean 6 Sigma Consultores provides high-quality consulting services delivered by experienced international consultants. The firm offers tailored guidance and methodologies designed to address the specific needs of each client organization. Its approach is based on Lean and Six Sigma principles to improve processes, increase efficiency, and reduce waste. Team members collaborate closely with clients to implement sustainable improvements and support continuous development. This environment offers early-career professionals the opportunity to learn from seasoned experts and contribute to impactful projects.

Role Description As a Junior Consultant at Lean 6 Sigma Consultores, you will support senior consultants in analyzing client processes, gathering and interpreting data, mapping workflows, and identifying opportunities for improvement. On a day-to-day basis, you will support in the design and implementation of Lean and Six Sigma initiatives. You will also contribute to documenting project progress, tracking key performance indicators, and ensuring that agreed actions are followed through. The role involves continuous learning, including on-the-job training in Lean, Six Sigma, and project management methodologies.

Qualifications

Foundational knowledge or strong interest in process improvement, Lean, and/or Six Sigma methodologies.
Analytical and problem-solving skills, including the ability to work with data, identify patterns, and draw meaningful conclusions.
Strong communication and presentation abilities, with clear written and spoken English; Portuguese proficiency is an advantage.
Team-oriented mindset, with the ability to collaborate, take feedback, and contribute to a supportive project environment.
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Management, Economics, Business, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
Attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
Willingness to learn, curiosity about operational excellence, and openness to working directly at client sites when needed.
Familiarity with Excel, PowerPoint, and other productivity tools; experience with data analysis or visualization tools is a plus.

Title: Data analyst & dashboard designer
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data analyst & dashboard designer

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analytics, cybersecurity risk management and data visualization? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst & Dashboard Designer, you will be responsible for leading end-to-end dashboard initiatives within a large-scale international cybersecurity environment, contributing to Enterprise Security activities and cyber risk governance.

Build Side


Understand business requirements and identify relevant KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management
Design and implement robust data models to support cyber risk dashboards
Collect, clean and transform large datasets using ETL tools
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI and Tableau
Use Dataiku and PostgreSQL for advanced data processing and statistical analysis
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover hidden correlations and generate additional insights
Continuously improve data models to enhance accuracy, usability and performance


Run Side


Ensure data accuracy, consistency and availability across dashboards
Monitor data quality and proactively address inconsistencies
Present findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate with cybersecurity, IT risk and resilience teams across multiple locations
Manage multiple dashboard projects simultaneously with autonomy and strong time management
Contribute to the continuous improvement of dashboard production processes


This is your background

You have at least 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst in demanding, data-driven environments.

You enjoy transforming complex datasets into meaningful visual insights and are constantly looking for ways to innovate through data science and automation. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology goes hand in hand with collaboration.

This Is Your Expertise


Power BI & Tableau (dashboard design and advanced DataViz)
Dataiku (data preparation, data science workflows)
PostgreSQL (data extraction, transformation and querying)
ETL tools and data modeling best practices
Statistical analysis and basic machine learning concepts
Basic Python skills
Microsoft collaboration tools (PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams)


Certifications such as Tableau Certified Data Analyst or Dataiku Advanced Designer are a plus.

Languag Skills


English: C1


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Excellent communication skills in English (French is a plus)
Ability to collaborate effectively with distributed cybersecurity teams
Autonomous, organized and capable of managing multiple priorities
Proactive in identifying issues and proposing concrete solutions


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybjrid (50%)

Range: €39,000–€52,000 (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data / Cybersecurity / IT Risk

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data analyst & dashboard designer

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analytics, cybersecurity risk management and data visualization? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst & Dashboard Designer, you will be responsible for leading end-to-end dashboard initiatives within a large-scale international cybersecurity environment, contributing to Enterprise Security activities and cyber risk governance.

Build Side

  • Understand business requirements and identify relevant KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management
  • Design and implement robust data models to support cyber risk dashboards
  • Collect, clean and transform large datasets using ETL tools
  • Develop and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI and Tableau

Run Side

  • Monitor data quality and proactively address inconsistencies
  • Present findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
  • Collaborate with cybersecurity, IT risk and resilience teams across multiple locations
  • Manage multiple dashboard projects simultaneously with autonomy and strong time management
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of dashboard production processes

This is your background

You have at least 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst in demanding, data-driven environments.

You enjoy transforming complex datasets into meaningful visual insights and are constantly looking for ways to innovate through data science and automation. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology goes hand in hand with collaboration.

This Is Your Expertise

  • Power BI & Tableau (dashboard design and advanced DataViz)
  • Dataiku (data preparation, data science workflows)
  • PostgreSQL (data extraction, transformation and querying)
  • ETL tools and data modeling best practices
  • Statistical analysis and basic machine learning concepts
  • Basic Python skills
  • Microsoft collaboration tools (PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams)

Certifications such as Tableau Certified Data Analyst or Dataiku Advanced Designer are a plus.

Languag Skills

This is how you work as part of a team:

  • Ability to collaborate effectively with distributed cybersecurity teams
  • Autonomous, organized and capable of managing multiple priorities
  • Proactive in identifying issues and proposing concrete solutions

Title: Procurement Data and Insight Analyst
Description:
Description

The Procurement Data and Insight Analyst plays a key role in supporting Unilabs’ procurement operations through data analysis, reporting, and actionable insights. This role requires a strong understanding of procurement processes, data analysis methods, and business intelligence tools. The successful candidate will work closely with the procurement team to optimize strategies, identify savings opportunities, and monitor supplier performance.




About the role

The Procurement Data and Insight Analyst is responsible for building and maintaining analytical tools and intelligence systems that support procurement planning and operations across Europe. This is primarily a technical role focused on dashboards, automated reporting, and monitoring frameworks that help the procurement team act proactively on contract risks, savings opportunities, and process bottlenecks.

The successful candidate will work at the intersection of data engineering, procurement strategy, and operational excellence, translating complex spend data into clear, actionable insights for senior stakeholders.




Key responsibilities

Tool development & BI ownership core focus

Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards and automated reports to support procurement planning and decision-making
Develop and manage monitoring frameworks that generate real-time alerts for contract expirations, process delays, and sourcing gaps
Build and maintain Excel-based tools using automation features such as Power Query or VBA for ad hoc analysis and modelling
Ensure data quality, refresh reliability, and clear documentation across all developed tools




Procurement intelligence & monitoring core focus

Identify spend categories not covered by active contracts and prioritize them for negotiation cycles
Maintain a live view of the contract portfolio, including renewal timelines, savings tracking, and compliance status
Flag delayed procurement processes and escalate them to the relevant stakeholders in a timely manner
Produce regular spend analyses to identify trends, maverick buying, and cost-saving opportunities across categories




Data analysis & reporting

Collect, structure, and interpret large procurement datasets to identify patterns and improvement opportunities
Define and maintain KPIs, SLAs, and performance benchmarks across the procurement function
Provide ad hoc analysis and reporting to support data-driven decisions by the leadership team




Supplier performance & strategy

Monitor supplier performance against defined KPIs and SLAs, identify gaps, and recommend corrective actions
Conduct market research and supplier analysis to support category strategies and sourcing decisions
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including finance, operations, and legal, to align procurement insights with business priorities




Process improvement

Identify opportunities to streamline procurement workflows and improve operational efficiency
Support the implementation of process standardization initiatives across markets

Requirements




Education

Bachelor's degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, Finance, Data Analytics, or a related field




Experience

3–5 years of experience in procurement, data analysis, or business intelligence roles
Proven experience building and owning dashboards and reporting tools; consumption-only experience is not sufficient
Experience in the healthcare, pharmaceutical, or diagnostics industry is a strong advantage




Technical skills

Hands-on experience developing in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and Power Query, is required
Advanced Excel skills, including Power Query and VBA automation, are required
Experience with Tableau or equivalent BI tools is a plus
Experience with Coupa or similar procurement/P2P platforms (e.g., SAP Ariba, Jaggaer) is required
Ability to build automated reporting pipelines and alert mechanisms from structured procurement data
Familiarity with SQL or Python for data extraction and transformation is a plus




Skills & competencies

Strong analytical and structured problem-solving skills, with the ability to work with large and complex datasets
Clear and direct communication skills, with the ability to translate data into executive-level insights
High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage competing priorities
Collaborative and cross-functional mindset, with the ability to work effectively across finance, operations, and legal teams




Languages

Fluency in English is mandatory, as it is the working language of the team
Proficiency in Portuguese, French, Spanish, or another European language is a strong advantage given Unilabs’ pan-European footprint

Title: Business Analyst | SQL
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.




We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We would like to meet a Business Analyst | SQL for a project with a hybrid working model in Porto, Portugal.




Responsibilities

To be a point of contact for users and other IT teams for any functional topic.
Investigate functional issues related to daily activity.
Document in clear and structured way production issues, their analysis, and solutions.
Discuss requirements specifications with business users.
Specify and document user requirements.
Specify and lead the implementation of changes in the system to meet requirements needs.
Respond to users requirements and interact with different stakeholders at different levels.
Create and modify reports/data extractions according to user needs using for the effect SQL and system internal tools.
Drive and foster the agility on the team organization.
Lead functional requirements gathering sessions with the business: Front Office, Finance, Operations, and Compliance. Document Business Processes: write business requirements documents and functional requirements (use cases, flow diagrams, etc.) and validate them with the business.
Assist in the review of functional requirements with IT.
Design and execute QA/UAT test plans in cooperation with business and support functions.
Work with users to identify issues and work closely with developers to define optimal solutions.




Requirements

Minimum 4 years of professional experience as a Business Analyst or similar role.
Degree in: Computer Engineering, Finance, Economics, Accounting or a related field.
Strong understanding of: Process design, Process implementation and Testing methodologies.
Strong experience with Agile methodologies (Jira is a plus).
Good SQL skills.
Experience with current-state operating model analysis, data reconciliation, quality review, workflow analysis, process improvement, strategic vision and planning and functional requirements gathering.
Ability to interact effectively with diverse stakeholders and users.
Excellent communication skills in English (written and spoken).
Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively within local and global teams.
Strong time management, prioritization, and multitasking skills.
High attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines.
Previous experience in the banking industry.







Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

Long-term projects with national and international context.
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust.
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




Join us

Send us your CV in English to mariana.azevedo@decskill.com with reference “MA/ITBA”.




Your next challenge might start here.




At Decskill, we are committed to equal opportunities and non-discrimination. We promote a culture of diversity and inclusion, where recruitment and career progression are based solely on talent, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality, or any other form of discrimination incompatible with human dignity.

Title: AI & Business Intelligence internship
Description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY:

ALTEN is a multinational group created more than 30 years ago, specialized in consulting services, information technology and engineering services to the main companies in the market. We achieved a turnover of more than 4.14 billion euros in 2024 and now have 57 700 employees, 88% of whom are consulting engineers.

ABOUT PORTO & POSITION:

To support our European intensive growth, the Group has decided to build a Center based in Porto with the aim to provide administrative and financial support to the European subsidiaries. In this context, we are looking for a AI & Business Intelligence internship. This role combines finance operations with data, Power BI, and AI-driven solutions, providing strong exposure to innovative projects and decision-making processes.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Support budgeting, forecasting, and monthly closing activities,
Analyze financial performance (actual vs. budget, trends),
Build and improve dashboards for management,
Audit and standardize Excel datasets for Power BI,
Develop Power BI reports and dashboards,
Design AI tools to automate analysis and detect deviations,
Generate automated financial insights and KPI analysis,
Support users, testing, and documentation.

:Bachelor's or Master’s student (Engineering, Data, BI, or similar) eligible for an IEFP internship
,Good level of english
,Strong Excel skills
,Knowledge of Power BI / Power Query
,Interest in Artificial Intelligence
,Analytical, autonomous, and business-oriented

Title: Graduate Programme 2027: Data Scientist and Analyst
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Graduate Programme

Our Graduate Programme is built for ambitious graduates who want to launch their careers in fintech. Over this 12-month programme, you’ll work on real projects, solve complex challenges, and make an impact from day one. With structured training and hands-on experience, you’ll grow your skills while exploring different areas of the business.

Top performers can be promoted during or after the programme, giving you the chance to continue your journey at Revolut.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Scientists and Analysts work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for Graduate Data Scientists and Analysts who are analytical, curious, and ready to deliver impact with data.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let’s get in touch.

Join us from anywhere

The Graduate Programme is open to international applicants willing to relocate to one of our tech hubs in Poland, Portugal, Spain, the UK, or the UAE. We’ll support you with visa assistance, travel costs, and accommodation (conditions apply). You’ll be expected to spend around 3 days per week in the office (where role and location allow).

Key dates


Applications: open from May 2026
Recruitment process: July–December 2026
Programme start: early 2027 (January–June) or late 2027 (July–December)


Roles are filled on a rolling basis, so apply early. You can specify your preferred intake during the recruitment process.

What You'll Be Doing


Taking part in a hands-on bootcamp and working on real projects across multiple teams
Helping to build analytical tools and machine learning models (such as dashboards and ETLs) to support product launches
Improving visibility in user sign-up flows and operational funnels
Enhancing and maintaining data infrastructure (including ETLs and LookML) to keep data accessible and reliable
Collaborating with teams across Operations, Growth, and Product to refine features and uncover insights
Supporting decision-making with ad-hoc analysis (e.g., interest rate sensitivity, incentive profitability, conversion rates)
Learning from industry leaders and staying aligned with team priorities through weekly meetings
Managing tasks, tracking progress, and communicating key outcomes
Sharing updates with our global teams
Receiving regular feedback to guide your development


What You'll Need


A predicted or achieved 2:1 (or equivalent grade) in computer science, maths, physics, or a related field
To have graduated in 2025, 2026, or 2027
Impressive Python and SQL skills
A solid foundation in maths and statistics
An interest in analytics engineering, product analytics, data science, or ML engineering
Great problem-solving skills and a proactive mindset
Fluency in English and excellent communication skills
Full-time availability from early or late 2027
The ability to attend a Revolut office in your country of employment at least 3 days per week (this is a hybrid role)


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Internship Programme 2027: Data Scientist and Analyst
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Internship Programme

Our Internship Programme is designed for ambitious students who want to make their summer count. Over 8–10 paid weeks, you’ll work on real projects, tackle meaningful challenges, and develop your skills alongside industry experts. It’s an opportunity to learn, innovate, and build a solid foundation for your career.

Top performers may be offered a place on our Graduate Programme the following year, giving you the opportunity to return and continue your journey at Revolut.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Scientists and Analysts work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for Data Scientist and Analyst Interns who believe no data task is too complex. You’ll dig into databases, uncover root causes, design smart solutions, and write the code to bring them to life.

Up to shape what’s next in finance? Let’s get in touch.

Join us from anywhere

The Internship Programme is open to international applicants ready to relocate to one of our tech hubs in Poland, Portugal, the UAE, or the UK. We’ll support you with visa assistance, travel costs, and accommodation (conditions apply). You’ll be expected to spend around 3 days per week in the office (where role and location allow).

Key dates


Applications: open from May 2026
Recruitment process: July–December 2026
Programme start: June / July 2027


Roles are filled on a rolling basis — apply early.

What You'll Be Doing


Taking part in a hands-on bootcamp and working on real projects across multiple teams
Helping to build analytical tools and machine learning models (such as dashboards and ETLs) to support product launches
Improving visibility in user sign-up flows and operational funnels
Enhancing and maintaining data infrastructure (including ETLs and LookML) to keep data accessible and reliable
Collaborating with teams across Operations, Growth, and Product to refine features and uncover insights
Supporting decision-making with ad-hoc analysis (e.g., interest rate sensitivity, incentive profitability, conversion rates)
Learning from industry leaders and staying aligned with team priorities through weekly meetings
Managing tasks, tracking progress, and communicating key outcomes
Sharing updates with our global teams
Receiving regular feedback to guide your development


What You'll Need


To be in your penultimate year of a bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, maths, physics, or a similar field (graduating in 2028)
Impressive Python and SQL skills
An interest or experience in analytics engineering, product analytics, data science, or ML engineering
A solid foundation in maths and statistics
A proactive, results-driven mindset
Fluency in English
Great communication, organisational, and problem-solving skills
To be comfortable working in a fast-paced, diverse team environment
Full-time availability for summer 2027
The ability to attend a Revolut office in your country of employment at least 3 days per week (this is a hybrid role)


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

Title: MDM Analyst
Description:
The MDM Analyst role will be responsible for the creation and maintenance of Master Data that is required for finance processes and is leveraged by cross-functional teams under RTR, PTP, OTC, FPA, FPR.

The role requires working closely with both Finance process users and everyday system users to create and maintain data at the expected service and quality levels. This role interacts with a wide range of internal stakeholders including other internal and external finance & non-finance teams, senior accountants, and local business finance leads. The role will support the business by understanding and implementing improvements, will assist standardization efforts, while moving towards to standard Manpower Group processes and procedures, supporting MDM Supervisors on improvements. Additionally, the MDM Analyst will work collaboratively with internal and cross-functional Manpower Group teams at various levels to fulfill responsibilities. This role requires strong understanding of Master Data Management processes.




Core Responsabilities:




Responsible for the day-to-day Financial Planning, Accounting, Customer, Supplier & Vendor Master Data.
Collate and action requests for data creation and maintenance.
Maintain chart of accounts (including GL, cost centers and entities), other accounting master data objects, customer records and contracts data.
Apply subject matter knowledge to improve Master Data Management processes including, but not limited to, maintenance of chart of accounts, setting up new customers in system, enabling customers and vendors on invoicing and procurement portals.
Perform Master Data tasks with accuracy and completeness, for example, to always enter and maintain complete and comprehensive details in the systems.
Continually own and maintain required standard data documentation.
Apply a degree of independent decision-making in dealing with complex business and/or technical issues.
Collaborate with cross-functionals teams across various levels including the BPO.
Establish stable working relationships within Master Data team and with other departments/team members to ensure smooth ways of working and delivery of Master Data activities.
Anticipate data needs of other teams and proactively seek to respond / create solutions in advance of requests.
Collaborate with Finance process owners to adapt best practices and standards.
Collaborate with IT and Data & Analytics teams on data structures and data strategy.
Collaborate with other stakeholders to provide required data points.
Ensure data compliance & integrity.
Complete all required training and adhere to company compliance policies & procedures.
Abide by data quality audits, policies, controls, documentation of controls and procedures for good organizational data health.
Create reports and provide inputs for frequent and accurate reporting on data input, cleansing and maintenance activity to accurately monitor implementation of Master Data maintenance strategy and compliance with company policies and regulation.
Run reports to assess data quality and contribute to any testing & development in support of data quality improvement initiatives.
Validate data integrity during any data transformation and loading activities.
Analyze and monitor business processes that create erroneous data to support remediation of issues.




Requirements:




3 years of experience in similar roles.
Experience with Oracle or other industry-leading ERP a plus.
Fluent English.
Hability to work between 2pm-11pm.
